Rhode Island Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Rhode Island

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through Board of Dietetics Practice.

Endorsement fee
$75
Processing time
3 to 5 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised dietetic experience (most accredited programs exceed this with 1,000+ hours).

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

  3. Submit the Application and Fee

    Complete, sign, and notarize the official application. Submit it to the Department of Health with the $75 fee (payable to the RI General Treasurer) and a 2x3 portrait photograph.

  4. Complete Background Check & Verifications

    Obtain an original Bureau of Criminal Identification (BCI) report from the Attorney General's Office. Ensure CDR verification is sent directly to the Board.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$75
Processing time
3 to 5 Weeks
Renewal fee
$170
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (expires Nov 1 of odd years) + 20 CE hours

Required Documents

  • Notarized Application & Photo: Application form with a notarized signature and a recent 2x3 head-and-shoulders photograph.
  • CDR Registration Verification: Proof of active registration sent directly from the CDR to the Board.
  • Criminal Background Check: An original BCI report from the Rhode Island Attorney General's Office.

Board Contact

Board
Board of Dietetics Practice
Department
Rhode Island Department of Health
Phone
(401) 222-2828
Email
doh.licensing@health.ri.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $170. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years (expires Nov 1 of odd years) + 20 CE hours.

Rhode Island Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Rhode Island dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is a $75 application fee, which does not include the cost of the BCI background check.

When does the Rhode Island license need to be renewed?

Rhode Island LDN licenses expire on November 1st of odd-numbered years. The biennial renewal fee is $170.

Does Rhode Island use the title 'Nutritionist'?

Yes, Rhode Island uses the combined title of Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN) to license both professionals under one standard for medical nutrition therapy.

Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Rhode Island?

Most endorsement applications take 3 to 5 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
